an1.296:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads solely to disenchantment, to dispassion, to cessation, to peace, to direct knowledge, to enlightenment, to *nibbāna*.
an1.296:1.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one *dhamma* that, when developed and cultivated, leads solely to disenchantment, to dispassion, to cessation, to peace, to direct knowledge, to enlightenment, to *nibbāna*."
an1.297-305:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads solely to disenchantment, to dispassion, to cessation, to peace, to direct knowledge, to enlightenment, to *nibbāna*.
an1.297-305:1.3: Recollection of the *Dhamma* … recollection of the *Saṅgha* … recollection of virtue … recollection of generosity … recollection of *devatās* … mindfulness of breathing … recollection of death … mindfulness directed to the body … recollection of peace.
an1.297-305:1.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is the one *dhamma* that, when developed and cultivated, leads solely to disenchantment, to dispassion, to cessation, to peace, to direct knowledge, to enlightenment, to *nibbāna*."
